skill.name: pptx
skill.description: Create, edit, inspect, validate, and thumbnail PowerPoint presentations using HTML-to-PPTX and Office Open XML workflows.
skill.summary: Build and modify PowerPoint decks with HTML, OOXML, validation, thumbnails, and template-aware editing.
skill.author: 92Bilal26

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, filesystem, network, and cryptography patterns; most documentation and schema hits are false positives from OOXML examples, XML namespaces, template literals, and installation guidance. Confirmed risks remain because helper scripts extract user-supplied Office archives with extractall and invoke external converters on untrusted presentations, so the community skill should not be published without sandboxing and path-safety hardening.

high_findings.title: Unsafe Archive Extraction of Office Files
high_findings.description: Multiple helper scripts call zipfile.extractall on user-supplied Office files. PPTX, DOCX, and XLSX files are ZIP archives, so malicious entries with traversal paths could write outside the intended temporary or output directory.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly uses extractall on files supplied through the presentation workflow. The domain requires archive handling, but no path normalization guard is visible at these extraction points.

high_findings.title: External Document Converters Run on Untrusted Presentations
high_findings.description: The thumbnail and validation workflows invoke soffice, pdftoppm, and git through subprocess.run. Arguments are passed as arrays, which lowers shell injection risk, but parsing attacker-controlled Office and PDF content in external tools increases sandbox escape and parser vulnerability exposure.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess usage is legitimate and does not use shell=True in the reviewed snippets. The risk is still high because untrusted document parsing is delegated to complex native tools.

medium_findings.title: Broad Filesystem Write and Temporary File Operations
medium_findings.description: The skill creates output directories, writes XML and image files, copies presentation content, and writes inventory or replacement outputs. These operations are expected for PPTX editing, but they need workspace boundaries when paths come from user prompts.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The writes match the skill purpose and are not inherently malicious. Confidence is medium-high because user-controlled paths can still cause unwanted overwrite or data placement without guardrails.

medium_findings.title: Local File Rendering in Playwright
medium_findings.description: The HTML-to-PPTX converter opens local HTML through a file URL in Playwright. This is a legitimate rendering workflow, but generated or user-provided HTML may load local resources unless execution is isolated.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code intentionally renders local slide HTML and sets a temporary directory. I did not find evidence of credential exfiltration, but local-file browser rendering expands the trust boundary.

low_findings.title: Scanner False Positives in Documentation and Schemas
low_findings.description: Many external command, weak cryptography, C2 keyword, network, and obfuscation hits occur in Markdown examples, OOXML schema enums, XML namespace URLs, and JavaScript template literals. These are not evidence of malicious behavior by themselves.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review confirmed these examples and schema constants are inert content or expected standards metadata. They explain much of the static risk score but do not confirm malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Dependency Installation Commands Include Elevated Package Installs
low_findings.description: The skill documentation lists sudo apt-get commands for installing LibreOffice and Poppler. This is normal setup guidance, but marketplace users should not run elevated installation commands without reviewing dependencies.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The sudo commands are documentation, not hidden execution. The risk is low because they require explicit user action, but they are still privileged operations.

dangerous_patterns.title: zipfile.extractall Without Path Guard
dangerous_patterns.description: Archive extraction is performed without visible checks that each ZIP member remains inside the destination directory.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: These exact locations call extractall as part of document processing. I did not see safe member path validation near the reviewed calls.

dangerous_patterns.title: subprocess.run for Office Conversion and Diff Tools
dangerous_patterns.description: External commands are used for validation, PDF conversion, image conversion, and text diffs. They use argument arrays, but still execute local binaries against user-controlled document content.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The calls are semantically required for the workflow, but they are dangerous patterns in a community skill because inputs are presentation files.

content.value_statement: PowerPoint editing often needs precise layout control, template awareness, and file validation. This skill provides workflows for creating, modifying, inspecting, and checking PPTX files with HTML, OOXML, and helper scripts.
actual_capabilities: Creates new PPTX files from HTML slide layouts with PptxGenJS.
Reads and edits raw Office Open XML for slides, notes, comments, themes, and layouts.
Generates thumbnail grids to inspect presentation layout and visual quality.
Extracts structured text inventory from PowerPoint files for template analysis.
Rearranges template slides and replaces placeholder content in existing decks.
Validates edited OOXML files against bundled schemas and original document structure.
limitations: Requires local dependencies such as Python packages, Node packages, LibreOffice, Poppler, and Playwright.
Works best when the assistant can inspect generated thumbnails before final delivery.
Complex animations, embedded media, and unusual proprietary PowerPoint features may need manual review.
Processing untrusted Office files should be done in a sandbox because archive extraction and converters are used.

use_cases.title: Create Client Presentation
use_cases.description: Generate a polished PowerPoint deck from an outline, with custom colors, charts, speaker notes, and visual validation.

use_cases.title: Adapt Company Template
use_cases.description: Analyze a template deck, select matching layouts, rearrange slides, and replace placeholders with campaign content.

use_cases.title: Repair Existing Deck
use_cases.description: Inspect OOXML, fix broken slide content or comments, validate the package, and regenerate a working PPTX file.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple Deck
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 6-slide PowerPoint deck about [topic] for [audience]. Use a clear visual hierarchy, speaker notes, and a professional color palette.

prompt_templates.title: Improve an Existing Deck
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this PPTX file, summarize the current structure, improve slide clarity, fix layout issues, and provide a validated updated deck.

prompt_templates.title: Use a Template Deck
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this template PPTX to create a new presentation about [topic]. Build a slide inventory, choose suitable layouts, and keep the template style.

prompt_templates.title: Perform OOXML Repair
prompt_templates.prompt: Unpack this PPTX, inspect the OOXML for broken relationships or invalid slide XML, repair the issue, validate the result, and explain the fix.

output_examples.input: Create a quarterly business review deck from this outline and spreadsheet summary.
output_examples.output: A PowerPoint deck with an executive summary, metric slides, charts, recommendations, speaker notes, and thumbnail-checked layout quality.

output_examples.input: Update this sales template with new product launch content.
output_examples.output: A revised deck that keeps the original template design, uses matching layouts, replaces placeholders, and preserves slide order requirements.

output_examples.input: Find why this edited PPTX will not open correctly.
output_examples.output: A diagnostic summary, corrected Office XML relationships or slide content, validation results, and a repaired presentation file.

best_practices: Run presentation conversion and validation tools in an isolated workspace when processing files from unknown sources.
Generate thumbnails after each major change and inspect them for text cutoff, overlap, and contrast issues.
Read the relevant workflow guide before editing so the generated deck follows expected OOXML and layout rules.
anti_patterns: Do not skip thumbnail review when creating decks with complex layouts, charts, or tables.
Do not edit OOXML directly without validating after each meaningful change.
Do not run the helper scripts on sensitive or untrusted presentations outside a sandboxed environment.

faq.question: Can this skill create a PowerPoint from scratch?
faq.answer: Yes. It uses HTML slide layouts and PptxGenJS to build new PPTX files with positioned text, shapes, charts, and images.

faq.question: Can it edit an existing PPTX file?
faq.answer: Yes. It can unpack a presentation, edit Office Open XML files, validate changes, and repack the final deck.

faq.question: Does it support company templates?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes workflows for template inventory, slide selection, rearrangement, and placeholder replacement.

faq.question: What dependencies are needed?
faq.answer: Typical workflows need Python packages, Node packages, LibreOffice, Poppler, Playwright, Sharp, PptxGenJS, and MarkItDown.

faq.question: Is it safe to use with untrusted PPTX files?
faq.answer: Use caution. The skill extracts archives and runs document converters, so untrusted files should be processed in a sandbox.

faq.question: Can it guarantee perfect PowerPoint compatibility?
faq.answer: No. It validates and checks thumbnails, but complex Office features may still require manual review in PowerPoint.
